request.question: What would have to be true for Jahez to re-rate to your bull case?

events.text: ## Bott

om line up front

The completed analysis frames Jahez around a scenario-weighted expected return of
16.8%, which blends bull, base and bear cases and remains above the
15.0% hurdle even after weighting for downside

events.quote:
METRIC VALUE
Implied IRR at entry 17.1%
Mandate hurdle rate 15.0%
Scenario-weighted expected return 16.8%
Quantified risk score (1 = lowest, 10 = highest) 5.5 / 10
Mandate fit Pass
Shariah scree

events.text: — but the granular bull-case inputs (terminal growth, margin exit r
ates) are not itemized in my connected sources. What I can do is reconstruct, from the operating drivers in the filings, the specific inflections that would have
to occur for Jahez to re-rate to the upper end of that distribution. Five

conditions matter, roughly in order of importance.

## 1. KSA competitive intensity has to ease —

and monetization has to hold the gains

This is the swing factor. The Keeta-led price war drove
KSA delivery-platform revenue down 8.6% YoY as Jahez aligned delivery fees more competitively

events.quote:
Revenue declined 8.6% YoY as Jahez 
responded to evolving market conditions by aligning delivery fees more competitively and increasing its focus 
on commission - based monetization.

events.text: and group net income compressed. The bull case requires the pivot toward commission monetization to more
than offset the delivery-fee compression on a sustained basis:
commission revenue grew 16.3% to
SAR 1,113.8 million, offsetting a 13.1% decline in delivery-fee revenue, primarily as a result of compet
itive intensity in the Saudi market.

events.quote:
Commission revenue grew 16.3% to  1,113.8 
million, offsetting a 13.1% decline in delivery fee revenue, primarily as a result of the competitive intensity in 
the Saudi market.

events.text: The take-rate lever is the clearest re-rating
path. Take rate has already climbed —
continued improvement in take rate by +1.3pp YoY
reaching 16.15% in KSA in Q4

events.quote:

• Snoonuto serve as Jahez Group’s international arm for all non-KSA operations
• 18% gross profit margin supported by improvement in ads revenue, co

events.text: — and management explicitly flags that
lower current take rate provides structural
monetization upside, with Jahez balancing profitability with merchant success.

alizes, KSA orders re-accelerate (Q1-2026 already show
ed
market share recovery in KSA despite Ramadan seasonality, supported by targeted marketing and retention investments

events.text:
), and the higher take rate sticks rather than being competed away.

## 2. Non-KSA /

Snoonu turns clearly EBITDA-positive at scale

Non-KSA is transitioning from a large drag to a pot
ential contributor. The loss has already narrowed dramatically:
net revenue rose 118
.3% YoY to SAR 462.4 million while Adjusted EBITDA losses narrowed to SAR 14.4 million, with margin improving to
negative 3.1% from negative 26.5% in FY2024.

events.quote:
3 % YoY to  462.4 million, 
while Adjusted EBITDA losses narrowed significantly to  14.4 million, with margin improving to negative 3.1% 
from negative 26.5% in FY2024.

events.text: The acquired platform is itself already profitable —
Snoonu grew GMV 66% YoY to SAR 2.36 billion, expanded gross revenue 72% to SAR 904
.8 million, and generated profitable EBITDA of SAR 53.7 million.

events.quote:
Snoonu delivered a strong FY2025, with GMV growing 66% YoY to  2.3 6 billion and gross revenue expanding 
72 % to  904.8 million, underpinned by rapid user and engagement growth.

Snoonu generated profitable EBITDA  5 3 .7 million, 
reflecting disciplined cost management as the business scales.

events.text: For the bull case, the seg
ment has to cross into consolidated profitability. Management guided that
EBITDA for non-Saudi platforms is expected to turn positive within the next two quarters.

events.quote:
Our EBITDA for non-Saudi platforms 
has shown substantial improvement 
and is expected to turn positive within 
the next two quarters.

events.text: The upside optionality is the international rollout
executing cleanly: Snoonu as the platform for
initial rollout in Kuwait
and Bahrain starting with food delivery, with Bahrain launch in H2 2026, plus Oman identified as the next market.

events.quote:
Platform-led 
international expansion
 Snoonu to lead all 
international markets outside 
KSA
 Supports a multi-vertical 
growth strategy focused on 
engagement, retention, and 
wallet share

events.text: Kuwait has already soft-launched, so proof of the multi-vertical playbook travelling profitably is the mil

estone to watch.

## 3. Non-food / grocery verticals scale into a meaningful GMV mix

The di
versification story is inflecting fast.
Non-food GMV grew 4x in 2025 vers
us the prior year, lifting non-food contribution to GMV to 7% from 2%.

events.quote:
o Demonstrated high growth in t he non - food segment as GMV grew 4 x in 2025 compared to the 
prior year. In total, non - food contribution to GMV increased to 7% in 2025, compared to 2 % in the 
p

events.text: The capital-light grocery model
via noon is the accelerant:
Jahez KSA grocery GMV grew 2.5x
from Q1 2025 to Q1 2026, with 52% of that growth driven by the noon contribution — J
ahez earning commission on grocery GMV fulfilled and delivered by noon.

events.quote:

events.text: Bull case: this comp
ounds toward a double-digit GMV mix, diversifying away from price-compet

ed food delivery and lifting blended take economics.

## 4. Logi keeps comp

ressing per-order delivery cost

Logi is the structural cost advantage that lets Jahez discount without b
leeding. It reached
40% of Jahez deliveries by Q4
2025 with improving unit cost of delivery, expanding its fleet to over 4,000 sponsored drivers

events.quote: As a result, Logi now accounts for 40% of Jahez deliveries by Q4 2025 with improving unit cost of delivery.

events.text: , and turned
in
adjusted EBITDA up 87.6% YoY to SAR 8.5 million with marg
in doubling to 8.1% in Q1 2026.

• Bahrain & Kuwait – focus

ulfilment share keeps rising, driving the ~SAR 2 lower delivery cost per order that funds compet

itive offers while protecting contribution margin.

## 5. The one-offs don't recur and net income re-inflects

F
Y2025 profitability was distorted by non-cash and deal items
Net income compressed 61.2% YoY to SAR 73.0m, driven by SAR 55.4m of
Q4-2025 one-off items — Red Color impairment, Marn goodwill impairment, Others-segment ECL, and Snoonu de
al costs — layered on take-rate pressure.

events.quote:
Company Jahez
Sector Quick-commerce / food delivery
Stage Analysis complete (in Investment Committee review)

FY2025 GMV reached

events.text: The bull case simply requires these not to repeat, comb
ined with the H2 margin recovery management is guiding to:
profitability expected to improve sequentially across the year with
a stronger H2 profile, supported by cost optimization initiatives underway across the Group.

events.quote:
Gross Order Value including Delivery Fees and Value Added Tax

events.text:
## How this maps to the valuation

The DCF is built at
a
WACC of 13.31% with a 3.0% Gordon terminal growth rate, producing an implied IRR of 17.1
% at entry.

events.quote:
Methodology:
a discounted cash flow (FCFF) model, discounted at a CAPM-derived WACC of 13.31% (4.6% risk-free + 1.82x comp-set beta at a
5.0% equity risk premium, for a 13.7% cost of equity) with a

events.text: A bull-case re-rate is essentially the market
pricing in: (a) a higher sustainable EBITDA margin than the FY2025 exit of
8.3% adjusted EBITDA margin, down from 11

events.text: , recovering back toward the double-digit KSA level; and (b) a higher terminal growth assum
ption justified by the non-food/international mix shift. If FY2026 lands at the
top of guidance —
GMV of ~SAR 10.1
–10.8bn (+10–20%) and adjusted EBITDA of ~SAR 200–220m
— with the
one-offs behind it, the earnings base that today makes the
trailing P/E of
92.76x unrepresentative because of depressed trailing-twelve-month earnings

events.quote: Trailing P/E of 92.76x is distorted by depressed trailing-twelvemonth earnings (see Q1 2026 net loss, Industry & News Pack §3) — not representative of a normalized multiple.

events.text: normalizes,

and the multiple compresses on recovering earnings rather than on price.

## The

key caveat

None of this is guaranteed, and the near-term direction is the w
rong way:
Jahez swung to a Group net loss of SAR 9.2m in Q1 2026 vers
us SAR 35.3m net profit in Q1 2025 despite revenue growing 37.9% YoY, as operating
expenses rose on Snoonu consolidation and stepped-up marketing.

events.quote:
Following the Snoonu acquisition, we 
announced a clear vision for our international expansion and positioned Snoonu as the Group's 
core operating platform outside Saudi Arabia. Snoonu's multi - ve

events.text: Management is explicitly leaning *
into* spend, not away —
guidance reflects an aggressive go-to-market posture with
higher promotional and customer-acquisition investment.

events.quote:
Group net income attributable to shareholders of the parent company 
Actuals
2026 Net Income considerations:
• Higher D&A following Snoonu consolidation 
• Lower contribution from time deposit inc

events.text: So the bull case is fundamentally a bet that the current invest
ment phase is transitional and that KSA competitive intensity moderates in H2
2026; if the price war persists into 2027, the same levers that dr

ive the bull case invert into the bear case.

Would you like me to pressure-test which of these five conditions the
scenario-weighted 16.8% is most sensitive to, or lay out the corresponding bear-case triggers?
